needs_verification.note: Brand page lists 62g for the 13cm screw without specifying production batch; confirm against a review's measured weight before relying on it for a hard cutoff.

review_result.summary: Reviewers consistently praise the Aero Lite's low weight, calling it one of the lightest screws in its class and a natural pick for fast, high-volume placements on alpine routes where screw count matters more than raw durability. Durability draws more mixed comments: the aluminum tube holds up fine in soft to moderate ice but several reviewers caution against repeated placements in hard or rock-scraped ice, where the teeth are noted to dull faster than steel screws. Reviewers rate the starting bite and cranking speed as adequate but not as confidence-inspiring as Petzl's Speed thread pattern on hard water ice.

review_links.key_takeaway: Excellent weight-to-strength ratio for alpine racks; teeth dull faster than steel on hard ice.
Aluminum tube holds up well across seasons of moderate ice but isn't the first choice for sustained hard-ice pitches.

ambiguities: Petzl also sells a non-Light 'Laser Speed' model with a heavier steel tube; resolved to the Light (aluminum) version based on the candidate name.

review_result.summary: Reviewers weighted toward technical ice climbing rate the Laser Speed Light highly for its Speed thread pattern, which starts a placement with a single hand motion and cranks noticeably faster than the Aero Lite on hard water ice. The tradeoff is weight and price: reviewers note it's heavier than pure alpine-rack screws and the folding hanger adds a moving part that needs occasional cleaning when it ices up. Teeth and tube durability get uniformly positive marks even after multiple seasons of repeated placements in hard, brittle ice.

review_links.key_takeaway: Fastest starting bite in its class; heavier than pure alpine-rack screws.
Teeth hold an edge through repeated hard-ice placements with minimal wear.

run_notes: Both candidates resolved via official brand product pages.
Petzl Laser Speed Light (16cm) exceeds the 70g weight requirement (78g); flagged as a requirement violation rather than dropped, per v1 policy.
